title: elaeocarpus occidentalis
intro: Elaeocarpus occidentalis, also known as the Western Elaeocarpus, is a species of flowering plant native to Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and dermatitis due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus occidentalis include soothing irritated skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and benefits.
